text: 42.2g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for seeds, the fat profile is one of the biggest reasons they earn their place.
28.9g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for a seed, density is expected, so the quality has to show up too.
18.3g of protein. essential amino acid support is one of the better parts here. protein is a bonus, but not enough by itself.
vitamin e at 2% daily value stands out most. that is barely moving the needle.
magnesium at 95% daily value, zinc at 44% daily value, iron at 32% daily value stand out most. that gives this food real mineral support.

pros first: absurd omega-3 density because this should threaten the top of the seeds category. extreme fibre density because another elite seed trait. strong mineral support because very hard to dismiss nutritionally.

now the downsides: best used ground/processed appropriately because whole-seed use can reduce payoff. not universally loved because practical downside. can be rough for some because very high fibre is not free for everyone.
S tier. Best as a support food that boosts meals instead of carrying them alone.
